title: Bootstrap alchemy v2 Cloudflare state store (one-time interactive)
description:
With the alchemy@2 migration + working CF API token, main's production deploys now fail with:

  AuthError: State store not found for script alchemy-state-store. Deploy the state store first.
    at node_modules/alchemy/src/Cloudflare/StateStore/State.ts:101:17

This is a new blocker class, exposed only after the auth fix landed (previously masked by 401s from the under-scoped token). It's a deliberate guard in alchemy v2:

  // TODO(sam): do we want to support bootstrapping the state store from CI?
  // for now - just die here

The state store provisioning flow (in alchemy/src/Cloudflare/StateStore/State.ts):

  1. Read profile credentials cache (~/.alchemy/<profile>/cloudflare-state-store)
     → if present, use it.
  2. Else, query Cloudflare for the alchemy-state-store worker.
  3. If the worker exists → loginWithCloudflare() (works in CI; uses the API
     token to read the secrets-store auth token via an edge-preview probe).
  4. If the worker does NOT exist AND CI=true → die with the error above.
  5. If the worker does NOT exist AND CI=false → interactive prompt; deploys
     the state store + secrets store + auth token.

The recommended remediation is a one-time interactive bootstrap from a maintainer's devshell:

  cd /path/to/stackpanel
  nix develop --impure
  bunx alchemy deploy --stage staging --yes        # or production
  # When prompted "Cloudflare State Store not found. Do you want to deploy it?" → y
  # alchemy creates:
  #   - Cloudflare Worker:        alchemy-state-store
  #   - Cloudflare Secrets Store: <single per-account>
  #   - Auth token in the secrets store
  # After this, every CI deploy on every branch can use Cloudflare.state()
  # because step (3) above succeeds.

The CF API token already provisioned (cfut_A8wV…) has all the scopes needed (verified
via curl probe: workers/scripts read+write, workers/subdomain, kv/namespaces,
zones/.../workers/routes, workers/domains).

Acceptance:
- One-time deploy of alchemy-state-store completed (verified via
  GET /accounts/:id/workers/scripts/alchemy-state-store returning 200).
- A subsequent CI Deploy Web run on main against --stage production succeeds
  through the Cloudflare.state() initialization step.
- README/AGENTS.md updated with the bootstrap procedure so future maintainers
  don't repeat this discovery.

Follow-up (longer term): file an upstream alchemy issue to support CI
bootstrapping (maybe via an explicit `bunx alchemy state-store deploy`
subcommand), so projects don't need a one-shot human interaction.

Refs:
- main HEAD: 0f95da6f (Deploy Web Run 25107110360 — failed with this error)
- comment in packages/infra/src/lib/deploy.ts:140-141 already documents this
  expectation: "deployed on first interactive use; CI relies on it existing"
close_reason: State-store Worker provisioned manually; CI deploys can now use Cloudflare.state() (verified by user 2026-04-29)

title: Restore .open-next/cache asset overlay once alchemy@2 supports AssetsProps.sources
description:
## Update 2026-04-29: deeper root cause + scope expansion

**Original framing** (cache overlay missing) was wrong. After the auth issues unblocked (PR #15 + #17), the actual Docs deploy failure surfaced and it's a structural alchemy@2 + OpenNext bundling incompatibility, not a missing-cache thing. Symptoms:

  UnknownCloudflareError: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot destructure property
  'name' of '(intermediate value)' as it is undefined.
    at worker.js:1:23445 in createGenericHandler

This happens during Cloudflare's Worker validation step (the platform spins up the script in an isolate to verify it parses + initializes). It rejects the upload before it ever serves a request, so the deploy fails hard.

### Root cause

`@opennextjs/aws/dist/core/createGenericHandler.js` calls:

  const { name, wrapper } = await resolveWrapper(override?.wrapper);

`resolveWrapper` defaults to `import("../overrides/wrappers/aws-lambda.js")`, with a comment that says "This will be replaced by the bundler" — meaning the OpenNext build pipeline expects its own bundler (esbuild via `@opennextjs/cloudflare`) to swap that import for the Cloudflare-specific wrapper at build time.

`apps/docs/.open-next/worker.js` is produced by `bunx opennextjs-cloudflare build`. That file is correct on its own — it's the OpenNext-bundled Workers module that wrangler happily uploads (cf. `apps/docs/wrangler.jsonc` already in the tree).

**alchemy@2's `Cloudflare.Worker`** then re-bundles `props.main` through `@distilled.cloud/cloudflare-rolldown-plugin` (see `node_modules/.bun/alchemy@2.0.0-beta.20+…/node_modules/alchemy/src/Cloudflare/Workers/Worker.ts:1231-1265` — `prepareBundle`). Even with `isExternal: true` (which IS a real flag: `Platform.ts:23-29`, and skips the Effect-wrapping wrapper script), `buildBundle()` still runs rolldown over the input. That re-bundle either:

  - drops the OpenNext `import("./open-next.config.mjs")` dynamic import
    (so `handlerConfig` is undefined, then `override?.wrapper` is undefined,
    then `resolveWrapper(undefined)` walks the default branch and tries to
    import `../overrides/wrappers/aws-lambda.js` from a path that no longer
    exists in the rebuilt bundle); OR
  - tree-shakes / mis-resolves the swap that OpenNext's own bundler put in
    place at first build, so `m_1.default` ends up undefined.

Either way, `await resolveWrapper(...)` returns `undefined` and the destructure on `{ name, wrapper }` throws.

### Why this only surfaced now

Until today, every Docs deploy on every branch failed *before* the upload step:
  - Pre-PR #16: deploys used the old `alchemy-effect` API + 0.81-style Worker wrapping; the OpenNext-cache vendored overlay was in place.
  - PR #16 (alchemy@2 migration): introduced this regression but every deploy died with `Unauthorized` (under-scoped CF token) before reaching upload.
  - PR #17 + the cherry-pick on main fixed the token; main's deploys then died with `State store not found` (`stackpanel-8yl`).
  - PR #15's preview run was the first deploy in the alchemy@2 era to (a) succeed at auth and (b) bypass the state-store check (preview uses `localState()`). It's the first run ever to call PUT /workers/scripts; that's when this error surfaced.

So this is a NEW failure mode introduced by PR #16, not a pre-existing condition.

### Severity

Bumping to P0. Without a fix, no Docs deploy can succeed — neither preview nor production. This is independent from `stackpanel-8yl` (state-store bootstrap) but stacks on top of it: even after the state store is provisioned, main's Docs deploy will fail here.

### Workaround options (cheapest first)

1. **Bypass alchemy for the Docs Worker upload, keep alchemy for asset upload + custom domain.**
   `apps/docs/alchemy.run.ts` already imports `@distilled.cloud/cloudflare/workers` (it uses `Workers.listDomains`/`putDomain`/`deleteDomain` for the custom-hostname binding). We can call `Workers.putScript` directly with the pre-built `.open-next/worker.js` content + the assets binding, skipping `Cloudflare.Worker` entirely. Pro: minimal moving parts; alchemy still owns the rest of the deploy. Con: foregoes alchemy's typing/lifecycle for the Worker itself; need to manually wire bindings.

2. **Replace the Docs deploy with `bunx wrangler deploy` in `.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml`.**
   `apps/docs/wrangler.jsonc` is already current and correct (compatibility flags, `assets.directory`, `assets.binding`, `not_found_handling`, `html_handling`). Custom hostname can be set via `wrangler.jsonc#routes` or `bunx wrangler deployments create`. Pro: smallest code change, uses the supported toolchain. Con: docs deploys diverge from web/api (which still use alchemy@2).

3. **File upstream and wait.**
   File an issue against alchemy@2 for "support pre-bundled OpenNext-style Workers without re-bundling". Probably the actual fix is a `props.bundle: false` opt-out or honoring `isExternal` more aggressively. Pro: long-term correct. Con: blocks Docs deploys indefinitely.

### Acceptance

- One Docs deploy reaches a healthy serving state on `docs.stackpanel.com` (production) and `docs.<pr-N>.stackpanel.com` (preview).
- The chosen workaround is documented in `apps/docs/alchemy.run.ts` (or replaced by wrangler) so the next maintainer doesn't re-discover this.
- A separate issue is filed if the fix is "wait for upstream alchemy".

### Refs

- alchemy@2 source: `node_modules/.bun/alchemy@2.0.0-beta.20+1f3167790a4c81b5/node_modules/alchemy/src/Cloudflare/Workers/Worker.ts:1231-1265`
- OpenNext source: `node_modules/.bun/@opennextjs+aws@3.10.1+44325935d690066f/node_modules/@opennextjs/aws/dist/core/{createGenericHandler.js,resolve.js}`
- Last green Docs deploy: `514265ca` (PR #16 branch tip, stage `pr-16` → localState, but pre-merge so it never re-bundled the .open-next output the same way main does).
- Failed deploys after PR #16 merge: `66b3e57b`, `d97359fb` (auth-masked), `cf01361c`, `0f95da6f` (state-store-masked).
- PR #15 head deploy (auth + state-store both clear): `7b62e2c2` — first run to surface the `createGenericHandler` error.

### Remaining `stackpanel-49t` original scope (asset cache overlay)

Still valid, but downgraded to "follow-up after the deploy actually works". When alchemy@2 grows back the `AssetsProps.sources` API, restore the `.open-next/cache` overlay per the comments in `apps/docs/alchemy.run.ts:55-66`.
notes:
---

## 2026-04-29: Upstream patch ready

**State-store** (`stackpanel-8yl`): user bootstrapped manually. Deploy Web re-run on main commit `0f95da6f` is now ✅ success — confirmed prod deploy path works once the state-store Worker exists.

**Docs blocker (this issue)**: patch prepared on a fork branch of `alchemy-run/alchemy-effect`, ready for the user to open upstream.

- Repo: `git@github.com:alchemy-run/alchemy-effect.git` (cloned at `~/git/darkmatter/alchemy-effect`).
- Branch: `claude/cf-worker-bundle-false` (1 commit on top of `main`).
- Patch file: `~/.cursor/worktrees/stackpanel/xrm9/scratch/alchemy-patches/0001-feat-cloudflare-Worker-add-bundle-false-to-upload-ma.patch`
- Files touched:
  - `packages/alchemy/src/Cloudflare/Workers/Worker.ts` (+47/-0)
  - `packages/alchemy/test/Cloudflare/Workers/PrepareBundle.test.ts` (new, 108 lines)
  - `packages/alchemy/test/Cloudflare/Workers/preBundledWorker.mjs` (new, 15 lines, fixture)

**Design**: adds `WorkerProps.bundle?: boolean` (default `true`). When `false`, `prepareBundle` reads `props.main` directly and returns a synthetic single-file `Bundle.BundleOutput` (no rolldown). This is purely additive — existing callers are unaffected.

**Test**: integration test deploys the hand-written ESM fixture and asserts `worker.hash?.bundle === sha256(sourceBytes)`, which is only satisfiable when alchemy skipped the rolldown step. Also round-trips a request through the live Worker.

**Typecheck**: `bunx tsc -b packages/alchemy/tsconfig.test.json` passes on the patched files. Tip-of-main has 4 pre-existing TS errors in `test/Sidecar/{TestClient,TestServer}.ts` unrelated to this patch (verified with patch stashed away).

**To unblock stackpanel here**: once the upstream PR merges and a new alchemy beta is published, bump the `alchemy` catalog version, set `bundle: false` in `apps/docs/alchemy.run.ts`, and uncomment the `assets.sources` cache overlay (or migrate to whatever asset-cache API alchemy@2 settles on).

comments.author: Cooper Maruyama
comments.text:
Severity bump from regression-only to hard breakage. With current state, every Docs deploy on every branch (including main) fails Worker create with:

  UnknownCloudflareError: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot destructure property 'name' of '(intermediate value)' as it is undefined.
    at worker.js:1:23445 in createGenericHandler

Latest reproductions:
- main @ d97359fb (chore: rekey)              — failed
- main @ 66b3e57b (Merge PR #16 alchemy@2)    — failed
- claude/demo-via-project-swap @ 7b62e2c2     — failed (Run 25106523090)

Web deploys are unaffected and pass cleanly with the rotated CF token + regenerated embedded payload (see PR #15). Only Docs is broken, and the trace points at the Worker bundle itself, not at alchemy/CF auth — supporting Option B/C over A: even if upstream alchemy ships AssetsProps.sources, the createGenericHandler/DO-name destructure error is a separate failure to chase. Recommend bisecting the Docs Worker build (.open-next/worker.js) between the last-green Docs deploy and the alchemy@2 migration to find the exact regression site.

title: codegen: regenerate embedded SOPS payloads on .sops.yaml edit + rekey
description:
The runtime alchemy deploy reads from the codegen-emitted module
\`packages/gen/env/src/runtime/generated-payloads/_envs/deploy.ts\` (and its
companion \`data/_envs/deploy.sops.json\`), not from
\`.stack/secrets/vars/shared.sops.yaml\` directly.

Today, those embedded payloads only get regenerated when the devshell hook
runs codegen. That means edits to the source SOPS YAML — including the
common \`chore: rekey\` flow and ad-hoc \`sops set\` rotations — silently
drift from what production deploys actually use. We hit this in PR-15 and
PR-17: the Cloudflare API token rotation went into the source YAML and was
even merged to main, but the embedded payload at HEAD continued decrypting
to the old, under-scoped token, so every CI deploy after the rotation kept
401-ing on \`Cloudflare.Worker\` create.

Fix options to consider (pick one in design):

- Make the SOPS edit path (custom \`sops\` wrapper / hook) auto-run codegen
  for any \`packages/gen/env/data/**.sops.*\` or \`.stack/secrets/**.sops.*\`
  edit, and refuse to commit if the embedded payloads are stale.
- Add a pre-commit hook that re-runs codegen and stages the resulting
  embedded files (mirrors the existing oxlint/format hook surface).
- Or move the runtime to read the source SOPS YAML directly at deploy time
  instead of an embedded snapshot — eliminates the drift class entirely
  but is a much bigger change.

Acceptance:
- After any edit to \`.stack/secrets/vars/*.sops.yaml\` or rekey, the
  embedded deploy/app payloads under \`packages/gen/env/\` are guaranteed
  to be regenerated before the change can land on main.
- A regression test (or CI check) fails the build if the source-derived
  plaintext for any secret diverges from what the embedded payload
  decrypts to with the same recipients.

title: Fix broken bun install on main: alchemy-effect catalog reference
description:
Five packages reference `alchemy-effect: catalog:` (apps/api, apps/docs, apps/web, packages/db, packages/infra) but the root package.json#workspaces.catalog has no alchemy-effect entry. Result: bun install --frozen-lockfile fails with 'alchemy-effect@catalog: failed to resolve' on a clean clone of main.

Reproduction:
  rm -rf node_modules
  bun install --frozen-lockfile
  # error: alchemy-effect@catalog: failed to resolve (x5)

Root cause: introduced in commit dda9c459 'refactor: replace AWS EC2 infra with Cloudflare Workers + Neon, add agenix module' — the dep was added to packages but the catalog entry was never added.

Fix: add "alchemy-effect": "^0.12.0" to root package.json#workspaces.catalog (bun.lock already resolves alchemy-effect@0.12.0).

Surfaced while working on PR #15 — could not run vite to regen routeTree.gen.ts after route deletions, had to hand-edit. Worktree node_modules from before the regression still work, masking the issue locally.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #16 — migrated workspace to alchemy@2.0.0-beta.20 (alchemy-effect rebrand). bun install now resolves cleanly and lockfile regenerates without the catalog miss.

title: Add Hetzner provision regression test with ephemeral instances
description: Add a reproducible regression test for stackpanel provision using ephemeral Hetzner Cloud instances created on-demand via the hcloud API. The token already exists in SOPS as hetzner_api_key. The implementation should add the hcloud CLI to the devshell, create a disposable-machine test script, load hetzner_api_key from SOPS into HCLOUD_TOKEN, inject a temporary machine via .stack/config.local.nix, run stackpanel provision against it, verify the resulting NixOS host, and always clean up the instance.
design: Prefer a real end-to-end infrastructure regression test over mocks for the final provision path, but keep verification safe and deterministic where possible. Use existing shell smoke test patterns for script structure and use .stack/config.local.nix for the highest-priority temporary machine override.
acceptance_criteria:
- hcloud is available in the devshell
- tests/provision-hetzner-e2e.sh provisions an ephemeral CX22 in fsn1 from Debian 12
- The script exports HCLOUD_TOKEN from the SOPS key hetzner_api_key
- The script injects machine config via .stack/config.local.nix and cleans up in a trap
- Justfile exposes a command to run the regression test and a dry-run mode
close_reason: Implemented: added hcloud to devshell, tests/provision-hetzner-e2e.sh, and Justfile entries. Commit d54bdbc7.

title: P4: Green test matrix on stackpanel
description: Real deploys (not dry-run) of {docs,web} x {colmena,nixos-rebuild,fly} on stackpanel infra. Ensure docs and web have Nix packages that build to deployable artifacts. Deploy to ovh-usw-1 (direct) for NixOS backends. Add nix flake check validation for deployment outputs. All 6 cells must go green.

title: P3: Implement Fly as first plugin backend
description: Create nix/stackpanel/modules/fly/ with meta.nix (features.deployBackend=true) and module.nix. Register deployment.backends.fly. Inject app.fly.* options via appModules (appName, region, vm config). Generate fly.toml using pkgs.lib.generators.toTOML. Implement scripts.deploy (generate fly.toml, call fly deploy) and scripts.dryRun. Add fly config to stackpanel apps for testing. This is the litmus test for the plugin contract.
close_reason: Fly backend implemented at nix/stackpanel/deployment/fly/module.nix with Go CLI support in deploy.go.

title: P2: Port existing backends to script modules
description: Move colmena, nixos-rebuild, and alchemy from Go functions to Nix script modules. Identical commands, different packaging. Colmena: extract deployColmena() into modules/deploy/backends/colmena.nix. Nixos-rebuild: extract deployNixosRebuild() into backends/nixos-rebuild.nix. Alchemy: extract deployAlchemy() into backends/alchemy.nix. Verify identical behavior. Delete Go switch/case and backend-specific functions. NixOS business logic is FROZEN — same commands, same args.
close_reason: All existing backends (colmena, nixos-rebuild, alchemy, fly, cloudflare, aws) ported to modules under nix/stackpanel/deployment/.

title: P1: Define backend contract & scaffold generic executor
description: Define deployment.backends option type (registry with scripts.deploy/dryRun/status/validate). Define Nix args shape (pkgs, lib, app, backendConfig, machines, projectRoot, stateDir). Add deployment.specs computed option. Define context.json schema (dryRun, gitRevision, timestamp, env, targets, secretRefs). Refactor Go CLI deploy.go to generic executor. Update nix eval expression to return resolved specs.
close_reason: Backend contract defined in nix/stackpanel/modules/deploy/module.nix with deployment.backend enum. Generic executor implemented in apps/stackpanel-go/cmd/cli/deploy.go.

title: Pluggable deploy backends
description: Restructure deployment around pluggable backend modules. Backends become stackpanel modules that provide scripts (Nix-evaluated bash), the Go CLI becomes a generic executor, and Fly is the first plugin to validate the design. Test matrix: {docs,web} x {colmena,nixos-rebuild,fly} on stackpanel + nixmac dogfood. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/ for full design.
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch secrets agent writes to YAML instead of full config rewrites
description:
Replace the current secrets write paths that call WriteConsolidatedData or writeNixEntityJSON("secrets", ...) with YAML-oriented agent APIs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Secrets-related agent write paths no longer use ReadConsolidatedData plus WriteConsolidatedData for Studio-managed secrets settings
- Recipient and secrets-config mutations write YAML directly, using sops set where appropriate
- The old full-entity SetSecrets path is no longer used for Studio-managed secrets flows
- Server-side tests cover the new YAML mutation behavior and protect against regressions back to config rewrites
close_reason: All agent write endpoints (handleSecretsSet, handleSecretsWrite, writeGroupSecrets) now write to SOPS YAML. updateSecretsNix() is deprecated no-op.

title: Add YAML-backed storage for studio-managed secrets metadata
description:
Create the YAML storage layout for the secrets settings that Studio owns today, instead of persisting those settings under secrets in .stack/config.nix.

Acceptance criteria:
- Define the YAML files that will hold Studio-managed secrets metadata, including recipient groups, creation rules, and any KMS-related fields that should no longer live in .stack/config.nix
- Nix/runtime loading reads the YAML-backed data without requiring eval->JSON->serialize roundtrips of .stack/config.nix
- The design explicitly uses sops set for YAML mutations where encrypted YAML needs to be updated
- Existing hand-written Nix in .stack/config.nix is no longer the source of truth for Studio-managed secrets settings
close_reason: YAML-backed storage implemented in .stack/secrets/vars/*.sops.yaml with Go code in secrets_groups.go and sops.go.

title: Move studio secrets config out of config.nix
description:
Studio-managed secrets settings should stop mutating .stack/config.nix through eval-and-reserialize paths.

Use YAML-backed secrets metadata and sops set for YAML mutation so the Studio can update secret values and related secrets config without evaluating away user-authored Nix. Keep tree-sitter/AST patching as the only remaining strategy for code paths that truly still need to edit Nix source.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Complete backend adapters for Alchemy secrets and Fly deployments
description: deploy.go resolves alchemy and fly backends, but only a minimal Alchemy path is wired today and Fly is not implemented in the command dispatcher. Finish the hosted-backend adapters so the CLI covers the backends already modeled in config/docs, including safe secret handling and builder preflight where needed.
design: Preserve the current backend resolution rules (deployment.backend first, legacy deployment.host fallback) while moving secret handling into temp files or another non-argv mechanism.
acceptance_criteria:
- Alchemy deploy uses env-specific secret material without passing secrets on argv
- Fly deployments build/publish the expected container artifact and invoke Fly with clear preflight failures when Linux builders are unavailable
- Backend command construction is covered by tests or mocks
- Hosted-backend failures surface actionable, backend-specific error messages
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Add machine-target deploy flow to stackpanel deploy
description: docs/design/deploy-command.md specifies stackpanel deploy --target <machine> so operators can redeploy all apps assigned to a host, but the current command only accepts an app name. Implement the machine-targeted flow and align the no-arg listing/status output with that machine-aware model.
design: Reuse one config evaluation per command and derive the app set from deployment.targets rather than shelling out once per app.
acceptance_criteria:
- stackpanel deploy --target <machine> deploys every app mapped to that machine via deployment.targets
- Missing or empty machine targets fail with explicit errors
- No-arg deployment listing surfaces both machines and deployable apps consistently
- Add tests covering machine-to-app resolution and execution ordering
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Add Nix deployment output validation to flake checks
description: The repo already exposes deployment-oriented flake outputs through nix/stackpanel/lib/deploy.nix and nix/flake/global-outputs.nix, but I could not find matching check coverage that forces broken nixosConfigurations / colmena wiring to fail before someone runs a real deploy. Add validation so deploy regressions are caught during normal flake checks and CI.
design: Keep the deploy output path pure and reviewable; add checks in the flake/check layer rather than baking deploy-time behavior into runtime commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- nix flake check --impure validates the generated nixosConfigurations for configured machines (or an explicitly documented equivalent check)
- Broken deploy module wiring fails before runtime deployment commands are attempted
- Coverage includes unprovisioned-machine stubs vs provisioned hardware/disk layouts where relevant
- Any added checks are documented near the flake output wiring
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Finish structured deploy CLI flags and status output
description: apps/stackpanel-go/cmd/cli/deploy.go currently supports basic app deploys with --dry-run plus human-readable status, but the design doc calls for env overrides, machine-readable output, and richer status metadata. Finish the command surface so deployment can run cleanly in CI and other tooling without scraping terminal text.
design: Centralize deploy result formatting in deploy.go and keep non-interactive output first-class from the start.
acceptance_criteria:
- stackpanel deploy <app> supports --env to override deployment.defaultEnv
- stackpanel deploy status [app] supports --json with stable machine-readable output
- Success and failure paths include backend/target/env information without relying on TUI-only formatting
- Add Go tests for flag resolution and JSON/status serialization
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Complete the deployment feature
description: Deployment is partially implemented across Nix outputs, Go CLI commands, host-specific backends, docs, and the Studio deploy panel. The current repo has the core Nix scaffolding in nix/stackpanel/modules/deploy/module.nix and nix/stackpanel/lib/deploy.nix, app deploy/provision commands in apps/stackpanel-go/cmd/cli/{deploy,provision}.go, and a Colmena-centric UI in apps/web/src/components/studio/panels/deploy/deploy-panel.tsx. This epic tracks the remaining work needed to make deployment feel complete and coherent against docs/design/deploy-command.md and docs/design/provisioning.md.
design: Use docs/design/deploy-command.md and docs/design/provisioning.md as the design source of truth, but scope child tasks to repo realities already present in code.
acceptance_criteria:
- Break remaining deployment work into executable child issues
- Finish CLI, backend, provisioning, UI, and docs gaps
- Land a validated end-to-end deployment story for NixOS machines and supported hosted backends
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Generate MSW demo fixtures from proto-nix example fields
description:
Replace hand-written MSW handlers in apps/web/src/demo/handlers.ts with fixtures generated from the proto-nix schema 'example' fields.

Why: PR #15 (demo via endpoint swap) ships the MSW agent with hand-curated fixtures. These will drift from the real entity shapes as schemas evolve. The proto-nix system already supports an 'example' field on every scalar (see nix/stackpanel/db/lib/field.nix proto.withExample), but only nix/stackpanel/db/schemas/theme.proto.nix populates it.

Scope:
1. Migrate apps, services, variables, secrets schemas to mkSpField with populated example values
2. Add mkExampleData to nix/stackpanel/db/lib/ that walks a proto message and emits a JSON object using each field's example (or a deterministic placeholder if missing)
3. Add a Nix output (flake.packages.${system}.demoFixtures) that generates apps/web/src/demo/fixtures.generated.json
4. Wire MSW handlers in apps/web/src/demo/handlers.ts to consume the generated JSON; delete the hand-written fixtures we replace

Acceptance: demo fixture data is auto-derived from the same source-of-truth schemas the agent and TS client already use; adding a new field to a proto-nix schema makes it appear in the demo without touching apps/web.

Discovered while implementing PR #15 https://github.com/darkmatter/stackpanel/pull/15

title: docs.stackpanel.com production deploy throws CF 1101 at runtime
description:
Background: cloud-gate-foundation upgraded alchemy-effect 0.9.0 → 0.12.0. apps/web deploys cleanly to local.stackpanel.com. apps/docs production deploy succeeds at the CF API level but every request returns CF error 1101 (Worker exception).

What was tried (all merged in feat/cloud-gate-foundation, all still 1101):
1. Added `--yes` to the deploy step so the React-based Plan UI is skipped (this fixed a separate Symbol.toPrimitive bug; deploy now reaches the upload phase)
2. Pass full assets config object {directory, config:{notFoundHandling, htmlHandling, runWorkerFirst}} to mirror apps/docs/wrangler.jsonc — alchemy default for bare-string `assets:` was wrong
3. Pass `isExternal: true` to skip alchemy bootstrap that otherwise wraps `main` in `Layer.effect(tag, entry).asEffect()` — OpenNext exports plain `{ fetch }` shape

Bundle size from the upload phase: 40.62 MB unminified. CF Workers Standard limit is 10 MB compressed; 40 MB raw is at the edge.

Likely root cause (untested): rolldown bundle of OpenNext.worker.js inlines the dynamic `import("./server-functions/default/handler.mjs")` instead of preserving as a chunk, producing one oversized file that workerd refuses to fully load. wrangler deploy handles this differently (preserves chunks).

Possible directions:
- Split via rolldown output options on alchemy side (would require alchemy upstream change)
- Use a Build resource pattern to feed alchemy a pre-built bundle and skip rolldown entirely
- Leave docs on plain wrangler deploy (revert alchemy.run.ts for docs) until alchemy ships native OpenNext support

apps/web deploys via Cloudflare.Vite which uses viteBuild instead of prepareBundle — that path works.
notes:
Update 2026-04-24 (got CF token with workers tail scope from himitsu cloudflare-api-token):

Captured the original 1101 root cause via tail:
  No such module "node:perf_hooks". imported from "handler-BwC-NBMH.js"

Fixed by bumping compatibility_date to 2026-03-17 (commit on feat/cloud-gate-foundation). That date is when CF promoted node:perf_hooks to a native module — earlier dates make unenv try to polyfill it, but the polyfill itself does `import "node:perf_hooks"` so it cant substitute itself in a chunked bundle.

After fix: docs.stackpanel.com homepage returns 200, but /docs/* routes return 500 with a different exception:
  Failed to load external module shiki-db8d315635eb368c/core

Root cause (different bug): rolldown bundles props.main but Cloudflare Worker config has `unresolvedImport: false` (alchemy-effect/src/Cloudflare/Workers/Worker.ts:715). When OpenNext’s middleware/handler imports its own pre-bundled chunks like `shiki-db8d315635eb368c/core`, rolldown silences the unresolved-import warning, leaves the import literal, and the deployed worker fails to dynamic-import that module name at runtime.

Workaround paths:
- Drop alchemy for apps/docs and revert to `wrangler deploy` (the previous wrangler.jsonc approach). Trade-off: lose declarative cert/DNS, but unblocks docs.
- Patch alchemy/distilled.cloud rolldown plugin to NOT silence unresolved imports (would surface the issue at build time) and bundle shiki inline.
- Pre-process .open-next/ output before passing to alchemy so all chunks are inlined into a single file.

Recommend option 1 for now and revisit when alchemy adds a no-bundle / pass-through mode for `main`.

title: Studio: Marketplace browse + install panel
description:
In-studio UI for discovering, purchasing, and installing modules.

## Scope

- apps/web/src/components/studio/panels/marketplace-panel.tsx
- Sections: Featured, Official (stackpanel's own paid modules), Community, Installed
- Listing detail view: README, versions, pricing, screenshots (MDX + images from the listing)
- Install button: free → instant; paid → Polar checkout in a popup, webhook-driven refresh on return
- Installed view: updates available, usage (if module reports it), remove
- Calls into the CLI via agent-local endpoints for the actual install/uninstall (so studio doesn't need Nix directly)
acceptance_criteria:
- Browse renders paginated list with search
- Listing detail shows full MDX description + pricing
- Free install works without leaving the studio
- Paid install flow completes end-to-end without manual reload

title: Revenue accounting: gross / fee / developer net ledger
description:
Ledger that tracks every transaction with platform fee + developer share; source of truth for payouts and future reporting.

## Scope

- Drizzle table: revenue_event(id, license_id FK, event_type enum(purchase|renewal|refund|chargeback), gross_cents, fee_cents, developer_net_cents, currency, polar_event_id unique, occurred_at)
- Derived view: developer_balance(user_id, module_slug, balance_cents, last_updated) — sum of developer_net_cents minus already-paid-out
- Populated by the same Polar webhook handler as the license writes
- Dashboard API: /api/me/revenue — current balance, monthly breakdown, per-module totals

## Rules

- Platform fee: 15% flat at MVP, calculated at write time (can change later without rewriting history)
- Processing fee (~3%) absorbed from the 15% — developer always gets 85% of gross minus refunds
- Refund: negative revenue_event, reduces balance
- Chargeback: negative + lock payout temporarily (resolution flow is Phase 2)
acceptance_criteria:
- Every Polar webhook produces exactly one revenue_event (idempotent)
- developer_balance view reconciles to sum of events
- Refunds correctly decrement balance
- Dashboard API returns accurate per-developer totals

title: CLI: stackpanel install + Nix module resolver integration
description:
End-user install flow that adds a module to .stack/config.nix and wires it through the existing module system.

## Scope

### CLI (apps/stack-go)
- stackpanel install <slug>[@version] — looks up in catalog, checks license (for paid), fetches signed tarball, unpacks to .stack/modules/<slug>/<version>/, edits .stack/config.nix to add the module reference + version pin
- stackpanel uninstall <slug> — removes pin; leaves unpacked tarball for potential rollback
- stackpanel update <slug> — checks catalog for new versions, prompts to upgrade
- Handles paid flow: if no license, opens Polar checkout URL in browser, waits for webhook to create license, then continues install

### Nix integration
- New option: stack.modules.install = [ { slug = '...'; version = '...'; source = './path or fetchTarball'; } ];
- Resolver: if source points to .stack/modules/<slug>/<version>/, import the module's module.nix and merge into config
- Auto-detection from .stack/modules/ dir as fallback

## Why CLI-first

Studio panel can come later — CLI covers CI + power users today.
acceptance_criteria:
- stackpanel install <free-slug> works end-to-end
- stackpanel install <paid-slug> launches checkout, completes install post-purchase
- Installed module's options appear under stack.modules.<slug> in the config
- stackpanel uninstall cleanly reverts config.nix

title: Module tarball signing + verification
description:
Ed25519 signatures on module tarballs so installers can verify authorship + integrity without trusting the distribution CDN.

## Scope

### Publishing side (dev portal)
- Module author uploads tarball; cloud API signs it with platform key (MVP) OR author brings own signing key (Phase 2 with KYC)
- Store signature + public key id in module_version.signature
- Tarball hosted on R2/S3 with signed URLs

### Verification side
- Agent/CLI: on stackpanel install, fetch manifest + tarball, verify signature against catalog's published public key
- Refuse install on signature mismatch with clear error
- Nix side: evaluate tarball as a flake input with 'narHash' pin (Nix's own integrity check) once extracted — belt + suspenders

## Why signing even with HTTPS

- Defends against CDN compromise or MITM on corporate proxies
- Enables offline-verifiable attestation of who published what
- Precondition for any automated-update flow
acceptance_criteria:
- All published versions have a valid signature
- CLI install refuses on signature mismatch (tested with a tampered tarball)
- Signature check adds < 500ms to install flow

title: Module license + workspace entitlements
description:
License record granting a workspace the right to install a specific module (+ seats).

## Scope

- Drizzle tables:
  - module_license(id, listing_id FK, workspace_id FK, polar_subscription_id | polar_order_id, status enum(active|canceled|expired|refunded), seats int default 1, activated_at, expires_at nullable)
  - module_license_event(id, license_id FK, event_type, polar_event_id unique, payload_json, created_at) — audit log
- tRPC:
  - modules.listLicenses(workspace_id) — what this workspace owns
  - modules.verifyLicense(slug, workspace_id) → { valid, reason?, expires_at? } (called by CLI + agent)
- Capability JWT emission (reuses stackpanel-0bt infra): when agent requests a capability token, include 'modules': [{slug, version_pin}] in claims for all active licenses
- License inheritance across workspace forks / preview stages — TBD, default to no inheritance

## Enforcement touchpoints

- stackpanel install <slug> — CLI checks license before adding pin
- Shell entry — agent re-verifies licenses on devshell entry for installed paid modules; warns on lapsed
- Module-provided cloud endpoints — receive capability JWT, check 'modules' claim
acceptance_criteria:
- License auto-created on Polar webhook
- verifyLicense correctly reflects active/expired/refunded states
- Capability tokens include per-license claims
- stackpanel install refuses without a valid license

title: Polar integration: module products + checkout
description:
Wire each paid module's pricing to a Polar product; buy → checkout → webhook creates license.

## Scope

- When a module pricing row is created with model != 'free': auto-create a Polar product via @polar-sh SDK (one_time → product without interval; subscription → product with interval). Store polar_product_id in module_pricing.
- tRPC procedure: modules.checkout(slug, workspace_id) → returns Polar checkout URL. Called by studio/CLI.
- Polar webhook handler (packages/api/src/routes/webhooks/polar.ts):
  - subscription.active / order.paid → create module_license row
  - subscription.canceled / order.refunded → mark license inactive
  - Idempotent on Polar event id
- Success redirect to studio marketplace panel with a 'Installed' flow

## Notes

- Separate Polar products per module so fee accounting is clean and developers can see per-product revenue in Polar dashboard once they're onboarded
- Use Polar test env for dev; wire real keys via .stack/secrets/vars/common.sops.yaml
acceptance_criteria:
- Free modules skip Polar entirely
- Paid module checkout returns a working Polar URL
- Purchase → webhook → license row appears within 10s
- Refund → license marked inactive; install command refuses

title: Module catalog: Drizzle schema + tRPC search/list
description:
Foundation data model for the marketplace.

## Scope

- Drizzle tables:
  - module_listing(id, slug, name, summary, description_md, author_user_id, status enum(draft|pending|approved|rejected|deprecated), category, created_at)
  - module_version(id, listing_id FK, version semver, tarball_url, signature, manifest_json, released_at)
  - module_pricing(listing_id FK, model enum(free|one_time|subscription), price_cents, interval enum(month|year|null), polar_product_id, active bool)
- tRPC router packages/api/src/routers/modules.ts
  - modules.list({ category?, q?, cursor? }) — public, paginated
  - modules.get(slug) — public, returns listing + latest version + pricing
  - modules.listVersions(slug) — public
- Slug validation + reserved-word list (prevent 'stack', 'stackpanel', 'official', etc. without admin flag)
- Seed script with 3 stub listings for local dev

## Why first

Every other marketplace task depends on this catalog existing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Drizzle migration applied cleanly
- modules.list returns paginated results with search
- modules.get returns a full listing with pricing + latest version
- Reserved slugs cannot be used outside admin

title: Premium modules marketplace
description:
Epic: enable third-party developers to publish paid modules to the extension registry; users install/purchase from the studio or CLI; revenue flows to the developer minus a platform fee.

## Scope

- Catalog API, listings, search (free + paid in one lane)
- Polar-backed checkout for one-time + subscription pricing
- Per-workspace licensing with team seats
- Signed module tarballs, verification at install/shell-entry
- Developer portal for submitting and managing listings
- Revenue accounting + payout rails (Polar Connect / Stripe Connect)
- Manual review workflow at MVP; automated Nix static analysis later
- Studio 'Marketplace' browse panel; 'stackpanel install <module>' CLI
- Integrates with existing stack.modules option + nix/stack/modules/ auto-discovery

## Economics

- 15% platform fee (developer receives 85% net of processing)
- Processing (~3%) absorbed from the 15%, not the developer's share
- USD first; multi-currency later via Polar
- Monthly payout minimum (TBD — $50? $100?)

## Trust & safety

- Nix modules can execute arbitrary build code. MVP = curated launch partners + manual review + signed tarballs required.
- Phase 2 = automated static analysis for IFD, impure builtins, network calls outside known registries
- Phase 3 = restricted 'pure' DSL for modules that want a verification badge

## Relationship to other work

- Depends on stackpanel-9uo (protectedPaidProcedure middleware) — reuses the same gate pattern
- Benefits from stackpanel-0bt (capability JWT) for shell-entry license checks
- Module catalog shares DB with @stack/db; uses same Polar + Better-Auth plumbing

## Open questions (resolve before Phase 1 ships)

1. Platform fee: 15% sticker, or tiered (higher cut for top sellers / lower for new devs)?
2. Price floor/ceiling on one-time purchases?
3. Annual subscription discount rate?
4. Refund policy — 14-day hard return, or author discretion?
5. Licensing across workspace forks + preview deploys — each needs its own seat or shared?
6. Stackpanel's own paid modules (Hosted State, AI) — same catalog or separate 'Official' tier?

## Phasing

- **Phase 1 (MVP)**: curated launch, ~5 partner modules, manual payouts, honor-system + signature verification
- **Phase 2**: self-serve dev portal, Polar Connect payouts, usage-metered pricing, automated static analysis
- **Phase 3**: restricted DSL, plugin quality badges, bundled 'collections' pricing
acceptance_criteria:
- Developers can publish a paid module via dev portal and receive payouts
- Users can discover, purchase, and install paid modules from studio + CLI
- Per-workspace licensing enforced; non-licensed workspaces cannot install paid modules
- Revenue accounting tracks gross, platform fee, and developer net
- Signed tarballs verified at install; unsigned/invalid → refuse
- Curated launch with ≥3 partner modules shipped

title: Capability JWT: issuance endpoint + agent-side verifier
description:
Short-lived signed JWT from cloud API embedding plan claims, verified by Go agent before unlocking gated commands.

## Scope

### Cloud side (packages/api)
- POST /api/capability/issue → returns { token, expiresAt }
- Payload: { sub: userId, workspace_id, plan, seats, exp: now+1h, iss: api.stackpanel.com }
- Signed with Ed25519; public key checked into repo + embedded in agent binary
- Private key in AWS SSM (follows existing secret pattern)

### Agent side (apps/stack-go)
- New middleware capability.Verify that reads X-Stackpanel-Capability header
- Verifies Ed25519 signature against embedded public key
- On success: injects plan claims into request context
- On expiry/invalid: 401 with clear error

### Studio side (apps/web)
- AgentProvider fetches capability token on login, refreshes 5min before expiry
- Passes as header on every agent request

## Scope note

This is defense in depth. Primary gating is server-side (the tRPC middleware). Capability JWT matters only for local-only features we'll gate later (e.g., Pro UI panels).
acceptance_criteria:
- Pro-tier user successfully obtains and refreshes capability tokens
- Agent rejects requests without valid capability on gated endpoints
- Token expiry triggers clean refresh (no visible interruption in studio)
- Agent's embedded public key loaded at build time, verified with a unit test

title: HostedStateStore adapter for alchemy-effect
description:
Implement alchemy-effect's StateStore interface backed by the cloud tRPC router.

## Scope

- packages/infra/src/state/HostedStateStore.ts
- Implements the StateStore contract from alchemy-effect/State (get/put/list/delete)
- Uses @trpc/client with httpBatchLink pointed at api.stackpanel.com, Bearer auth via ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN
- Retries (Effect.retry with exponential backoff) for transient 5xx, not for 4xx
- Structured error mapping so alchemy's UX shows 'Subscription required' on 402 instead of a cryptic stack trace
- Feature flag via env: STACKPANEL_STATE_BACKEND=hosted|local; default local

## Integration

- apps/web/alchemy.run.ts and apps/docs/alchemy.run.ts: conditionally construct HostedStateStore when STACKPANEL_STATE_BACKEND=hosted
acceptance_criteria:
- Adapter passes alchemy-effect's StateStore contract tests
- 402 from API surfaces as actionable 'upgrade' error in alchemy CLI output
- Can deploy apps/web end-to-end with STACKPANEL_STATE_BACKEND=hosted
close_reason: Closed

title: tRPC router for alchemy state CRUD
description:
New tRPC router exposing state operations to the HostedStateStore adapter.

## Scope

- packages/api/src/routers/alchemyState.ts
- Procedures (all wrapped with protectedPaidProcedure):
  - get(stack, stage, fqn) → decrypted JSON | null
  - put(stack, stage, fqn, payload, expectedVersion?) → new version (optimistic concurrency)
  - list(stack, stage?) → [{fqn, version, updated_at}]
  - delete(stack, stage, fqn) → void
  - listStages(stack) → [{stage, resource_count, updated_at}]
- Input validation with Zod (stack/stage/fqn slug regex to prevent injection)
- Error mapping: 409 on version mismatch, 404 on missing, 402 on unsubscribed (from middleware)
- Wire router into packages/api/src/routers/_app.ts
acceptance_criteria:
- All procedures callable from a tRPC client with a Pro subscription
- Version conflict returns 409 (not 500)
- Unsubscribed caller gets 402
- Integration test exercises put→get→list→delete round-trip
close_reason: Closed

title: Drizzle schema for workspace_state with envelope encryption
description:
DB schema + KMS integration for storing alchemy state entries, encrypted per-workspace.

## Scope

- Drizzle migration: workspace_state(id uuid, workspace_id uuid FK, stack text, stage text, fqn text, key_id text, encrypted_blob bytea, version int, updated_at timestamptz). Unique on (workspace_id, stack, stage, fqn).
- New table workspace_dek(workspace_id FK PK, encrypted_dek bytea, kms_key_id text, created_at) — per-workspace DEK wrapped by master KMS key
- Helpers in @stack/db: encryptStateBlob(workspaceId, plaintext) / decryptStateBlob(workspaceId, ciphertext) using @aws-sdk/client-kms
- Lazy DEK creation on first write; DEK rotation hook (not wired to a schedule yet)

## Notes

- Plaintext state MUST NOT be persisted anywhere. Decrypt on read, discard after response.
- Master KMS key already provisioned via @stackpanel/infra — reuse that key ID.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migration applied cleanly to a fresh dev DB
- encryptStateBlob/decryptStateBlob round-trip correctly
- workspace_dek row auto-created on first encryptStateBlob call
- KMS decrypt calls only happen on read; no plaintext persisted
close_reason: Closed

title: Add protectedPaidProcedure middleware in @stack/api
description:
Middleware that wraps protectedProcedure and additionally verifies the user has an active Polar subscription at the 'pro' tier or higher. Rejects with TRPCError code FORBIDDEN (HTTP 402) + upgrade_url when the check fails.

## Scope

- packages/api/src/lib/middleware/paid.ts: new Effect-backed middleware
- Reads subscription state via the existing @polar-sh/better-auth plugin
- Passes {workspace_id, plan} into ctx for downstream procedures
- Unit tests covering: active pro, active free, trial, expired, no session

## Why this first

Unblocks every cloud-gated feature we'll ever build — Hosted state is just the first caller.
acceptance_criteria:
- protectedPaidProcedure exported from @stack/api
- Free/expired users get 402 with { code, upgrade_url }
- Pro users pass through with plan claims in ctx
- Tests cover all subscription states
close_reason: Closed

title: Hosted alchemy state backend (Pro tier)
description:
Epic: Build a hosted state backend for alchemy-effect (replacing the filesystem-only backend), gated behind a Pro subscription via Polar. Unlocks team deploys and fixes CI orphaning.

## Motivation

Today alchemy-effect ships only a filesystem state backend (noted in .github/workflows/deploy-{web,docs}.yaml). Our CI has to cache the .alchemy/state/ directory across runners so destroy jobs can find the resources to tear down — otherwise 'every runner starts blind and orphans Workers + Neon projects' (comment from deploy-web.yaml).

This is:
- A real pain felt today (CI workaround in place)
- Gate-able by architecture (requires our cloud API; cannot be reproduced locally)
- Natural fit with existing Polar + Better-Auth + Neon + KMS plumbing
- Already half-scaffolded: ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN is in packages/gen/env/src/effect/scope/deploy.ts

## Strategy

- Cloud API (api.stackpanel.com) exposes tRPC procedures for state CRUD, gated by a Polar subscription check
- Per-workspace envelope encryption (KMS master key wraps a per-workspace DEK)
- Custom StateStore adapter on the alchemy-effect side that talks to the cloud API
- .stack/config.nix option to toggle hosted vs local backend
- Studio UI panel showing state entries per stage

## Free vs Pro

- **Free**: local filesystem backend (status quo). Works fine for solo use.
- **Pro**: hosted backend. Survives CI runner churn. Enables true team deploys. Audit log of who deployed what.
design:
## Architecture

- Alchemy-effect StateStore is an interface (see alchemy-effect/State module). We implement HostedStateStore that calls tRPC procedures via HTTPS.
- tRPC routers live in packages/api/src/routers/alchemyState.ts with a protectedPaidProcedure middleware gate.
- DB schema (Drizzle): workspace_state(id, workspace_id, stack, stage, fqn, encrypted_blob, key_id, version, updated_at).
- Encryption: per-workspace DEK stored encrypted by KMS master key. On read, API decrypts DEK via KMS, decrypts state blob, returns plaintext. Plaintext never persisted.
- Auth: ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN is a signed capability JWT issued by cloud API after Better-Auth login, embedding { workspace_id, plan, exp }. Agent-side verifier checks plan claim before allowing mutations (defense in depth).
- CI: workflows pass ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN (from GitHub secret) → adapter uses it as Bearer → API verifies signature + plan.

## Subscription gate

protectedPaidProcedure middleware:
1. Resolve session via Better-Auth
2. Look up Polar subscription via @polar-sh/better-auth plugin
3. If plan.tier < 'pro' or status !== 'active' → TRPCError 402 FORBIDDEN with upgrade_url
4. Otherwise pass {workspace_id, plan} in ctx to procedure
acceptance_criteria:
- Pro users on Polar-active subscriptions can deploy using hosted state backend
- Non-subscribers get 402 from cloud API with clear upgrade link
- CI deploys no longer need the filesystem cache workaround in deploy-{web,docs}.yaml
- State is encrypted at rest (no plaintext blobs in Postgres)
- Studio has a 'State' panel showing deployed stages + resources per workspace
- Docs guide covers migration from local → hosted

title: Move .envrc generation into lib.initFiles (drop Go constant)
description:
stackpanel init currently hardcodes .envrc contents in a Go const (envrcContent in apps/stackpanel-go/cmd/cli/init.go). This duplicates nix/flake/templates/default/.envrc and means the template can drift from what ship-to-user init writes.

Action: add .envrc to lib.initFiles in nix/flake/exports.nix so it flows through the existing stepWriteInitFiles pipeline as a single source of truth; remove envrcContent + stepGenerateEnvrc from init.go; keep the idempotency semantics (don't clobber existing .envrc unless --force).

Context: during stackpanel-y53/0qu work (2026-04-23), the agent chose to keep .envrc on the Go side because lib.initFiles appeared broken. It was not — lib.initFiles works and already ships .stackpanel/{config,_internal,data,.gitignore}. It just doesn't include .envrc today, and its templateDir path (../flake/templates/default/.stackpanel) points at a nonexistent dir while the real templates live under nix/flake/templates/default/.stack and the repo-root .envrc. Fix that path drift too while you're in there.

title: add envrc generation to 'stackpanel init'
close_reason: Closed

title: stackpanel init should use 'gum' to guide user through setup with a nice tui. it shold be idempotent such that when new steps are added, users can just run it again.
close_reason: Closed

title: agent should become a systemd or launchd service and service restarts. it should also support multiple projects at once, currently its one agent per project. by passing the absolute path to the project in every request, it could support multiple projects. with a project ID sent in payloads to reference projects

comments.author: Cooper Maruyama
comments.text: agent should probably still run the way it does not via process-compose in development, rather than launchd

title: agent should publish a public api that allows you to do the same things you can do via the cli

rewrite proto nix lib so that defining defaults/descriptions isnt so awkward. dont force the positional API - allow an API which would be the new default that feels just like the canonical lib functions like lib.mkOption except they accept a proto index
close_reason: Closed

title: env-package: stale src/runtime/generated-payloads/registry.ts duplicates src/generated-payloads/registry.ts
description:
The Nix env-package emits a stub at `packages/gen/env/src/runtime/generated-payloads/registry.ts` (via `payloadRuntimeDir = "${runtimeDir}/generated-payloads"` in env-package.nix), while the Go codegen writes the real registry to `packages/gen/env/src/generated-payloads/registry.ts` (`generatedPayloadsRoot` constant in apps/stackpanel-go/internal/codegen/env.go).

The runtime imports use `./generated-payloads/registry` relative to `src/runtime/`, so they resolve to the stale Nix stub. Go codegen never overwrites it; only the sibling `src/generated-payloads/registry.ts` reflects the true per-app/env payload registry.

This is a pre-existing inconsistency (predates the multi-env fan-out work) but should be fixed: either drop the runtime/ subpath in env-package.nix and update loader/node-loader imports, or move Go codegen output under runtime/.
close_reason: Fixed: aligned Go generatedPayloadsRoot to packages/gen/env/src/runtime/generated-payloads so Go overwrites the Nix stub as designed. Orphaned src/generated-payloads/ tree removed; regeneration verified end-to-end.

title: P6: Studio UI + Docs
description: Expose deploy specs + state through agent API (read-only). Update Studio Deploy panel to consume backend-agnostic deploy specs and state. Add deploy/provision action triggers from panel. Unify deployment docs around pluggable backend model. Document how to create a new backend module (contributor guide).

title: P5: Dogfood on nixmac
description: Add stackpanel deploy config to ~/git/darkmatter/nixmac. Deploy nixmac web via fly — validate generated fly.toml matches existing hand-written one. Prove the module system works in a project that isn't stackpanel.

title: Audit and eliminate remaining non-tree-sitter config mutation paths
description:
Use the current audit as a checklist and eliminate remaining .stack/config.nix mutation paths that still depend on eval->JSON->serialize rewrites where path-level AST edits should be used instead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document every remaining .stack/config.nix write path and classify it as tree-sitter patch, intentional raw write, or migration target
- Convert any remaining fine-grained config mutation paths that should preserve source structure to tree-sitter patching
- Leave only intentional full-file writes or YAML-backed secrets writes in the exceptions list
- The final audit is captured in code comments, docs, or issue updates closely enough that the repo does not regress to eval-and-reserialize edits by accident
close_reason: No remaining non-tree-sitter config mutation paths for secrets. Tree-sitter/flakeedit only used for flake.nix inputs, not secrets.

title: Rewire Studio secrets UI to use YAML-backed APIs
description:
Update the Studio secrets screens so they stop calling useNixEntityData("secrets") for settings that are now YAML-backed.

Acceptance criteria:
- The recipient/groups/KMS secrets UI reads and writes through the new YAML-backed secrets APIs
- Saving from Studio no longer updates .stack/config.nix for Studio-managed secrets settings
- User-facing copy in the secrets UI reflects the YAML/SOPS-backed workflow instead of saying values are saved to config.nix
- Relevant UI tests or focused verification cover the updated save flow
close_reason: Studio secrets UI uses agentClient.readSopsSecrets/writeSopsSecret/deleteSopsSecret via /api/sops/* endpoints.

title: Unify deployment docs and migration guides around the current model
description: The repo currently mixes older provider-centric deployment docs (for example nix/stackpanel/deployment/README.md and apps/docs/content/docs/internal/deployment.mdx) with newer Nix-first deploy/provision design docs. Once the remaining CLI/backend work lands, refresh the public/internal docs so users see one coherent deployment story instead of parallel models.
design: Document the shipped behavior after the CLI/backend/UI work settles; keep examples aligned with docs/design/deploy-command.md and docs/design/provisioning.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Public docs cover app deploy, machine-target deploy, provisioning, and hosted-backend flows with current commands/options
- Internal/module docs stop teaching superseded provider/defaultProvider shapes where they no longer match shipped behavior
- Migration guidance explains old vs new config shapes and current container/deploy commands
- Examples and validation steps match actual commands in the repo
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Add stackpanel provision --new and config round-trip machine authoring
description: apps/stackpanel-go/cmd/cli/provision.go handles provisioning for machines that already exist in config, but the provisioning design also calls for a --new workflow that can author a minimal machine entry and preserve Nix path literals for hardwareConfig/diskLayout updates. Add that machine-authoring path so new-machine setup is not a manual edit-before-provision step.
design: Reuse the repo's existing config-writing/serialization patterns instead of inventing a new config mutator; add tagged path handling if necessary to preserve Nix path types.
acceptance_criteria:
- stackpanel provision --new <name> --host <target> creates a minimal machine entry in the canonical Stackpanel config
- hardwareConfig and diskLayout paths round-trip as Nix path literals instead of quoted absolute strings
- The provision flow can update the new machine entry after generating hardware config
- Add tests for config edit / serialization behavior
close_reason: Dropped: manual config editing is acceptable, provision --new deferred indefinitely

title: Wire deploy/provision state into the Studio Deploy panel
description: apps/web/src/components/studio/panels/deploy/deploy-panel.tsx is still Colmena-centric and does not appear to consume the CLI state tracked in .stack/state/deployments.json and .stack/state/machines.json. Update the Studio deploy experience so it reflects the same deploy/provision model and status that the CLI writes.
design: Expose deploy/provision state through the agent/web API rather than teaching the browser to read local state files directly.
acceptance_criteria:
- The Deploy panel shows machine provisioning state and last deploy state from the supported agent/CLI APIs
- Users can trigger deploy/provision actions from the panel with clear loading, success, and error states
- Unsupported or partially configured backends degrade gracefully in the UI
- Add frontend or integration coverage for the key panel states
close_reason: Superseded by pluggable-deploy-backends restructure. Work absorbed into new phase-based tasks. See openspec/changes/pluggable-deploy-backends/

title: Remove now-obsolete actions/cache@v4 of apps/{web,docs}/.alchemy from deploy workflows
description:
In the alchemy-effect → alchemy@2 migration (PR #16), all 5 deploy stacks switched from filesystem-based LocalState to Cloudflare-hosted state via Cloudflare.state(). The .alchemy/state/ directory is no longer used at deploy time.

The deploy workflows still cache it as a no-op:

  .github/workflows/deploy-web.yaml — Restore alchemy state (actions/cache@v4 on apps/web/.alchemy)
  .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml — Restore alchemy state (actions/cache@v4 on apps/docs/.alchemy)
  destroy job — actions/cache/restore@v4 of the same paths
  destroy job — Delete cached alchemy state (gh cache delete) cleanup

Plus the explanatory comment block above each cache step describing the LocalState pattern is now misleading.

Cleanup:
- Drop the cache@v4 + cache/restore@v4 steps from both workflows
- Drop the gh cache delete cleanup step in the destroy jobs
- Update or remove the now-misleading 'Persist alchemy's LocalState' comment blocks
- Verify deploy still works without the cache (the Cloudflare state store is the new source of truth and is self-bootstrapping per Cloudflare.state())

Should land after Cloudflare.state() is verified working in CI (depends on stackpanel-r7g / PR #16).

title: Docs: module author guide + marketplace policies
description:
Docs that make it obvious how to build, test, price, and publish a module — plus the policies that keep the marketplace trustworthy.

## Scope

### Author guide (apps/docs/content/docs/modules/)
- 'Build your first module' — scaffolding, module.nix structure, meta.nix fields, ui.nix if applicable
- 'Test a module locally' — stackpanel link (local dev), running against sample .stack/config.nix
- 'Package for publication' — tarball layout, signing, manifest requirements
- 'Price and publish' — free vs paid tradeoffs, pricing UX tips
- 'Get paid' — Polar Connect onboarding, tax docs, payout schedule
- 'Versioning + updates' — semver discipline, deprecation policy

### Policies
- Acceptable use: no crypto miners, no telemetry without disclosure, no license keys hardcoded
- Refund policy: 14-day no-questions-asked (author can opt into stricter)
- Takedown policy: security issues → emergency delist within 24h
- Revenue share + fee structure (the 15% sticker, transparent)
- Intellectual property: developer retains ownership, grants distribution license
acceptance_criteria:
- Author guide builds with apps/docs
- Policies are linked from dev portal's publish flow
- Sample module repo referenced from the 'first module' page

title: Module review workflow + automated Nix static analysis
description:
Prevent malicious or broken modules from reaching users. MVP manual, Phase 2 automated.

## Scope

### MVP: manual review
- Admin tool (packages/api route + studio admin panel) showing pending listings
- Reviewer sees: uploaded tarball contents, diff from previous version (if any), links to GitHub repo, automated scan results
- Approve → listing goes live; Reject → listing status updated with reason visible to author
- SLA target: 3 business days for initial review

### Phase 2: automated scans
- Static-analysis pass over module.nix + meta.nix:
  - Flag: import-from-derivation without explicit opt-in
  - Flag: builtins.fetchurl with non-allowlisted host
  - Flag: arbitrary path reads outside module dir
  - Flag: network calls during eval
- Feed findings into review UI; author sees them pre-submit
- Optionally: automatic 'verified pure' badge for modules with zero findings

## Why not AI review

Pattern-match is more reliable for this than an LLM for the boring 'did they try to phone home during eval' checks. LLM review can come later for README/security claims.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reviewer can approve/reject pending listings
- Rejected listings show reason to author with re-submit path
- Static analysis surfaces known-bad patterns in a handful of test cases

title: Developer payout: Polar Connect + KYC onboarding
description:
Pay developers their accrued balance via Polar Connect (Stripe Connect underneath), with KYC + tax form collection at onboarding.

## Scope

- Onboarding flow: first time creating a paid listing → prompt to connect Polar Connect account (redirect OAuth flow)
- Collect tax info (W-9 US / W-8BEN international) via Polar's Connect UI
- Payout job (scheduled): once per month, for each developer with balance >= $50, trigger Polar payout; record payout_event(developer_id, amount_cents, polar_transfer_id, status)
- Emails: onboarding done, first sale, monthly statement
- Admin tool for manual payout holds (fraud, chargeback disputes)

## Phase 1 fallback

If Polar Connect isn't ready: accumulate balances, issue manual Wise transfers quarterly while we collect via email. Works for ~20 developers, not for scale.
acceptance_criteria:
- Developer can connect payout account end-to-end
- Monthly payout runs successfully against test Polar env
- Balance decrements match transferred amount
- Tax forms captured before first payout

title: Developer portal: submit + manage listings
description:
Dashboard where authors create listings, upload versions, set pricing, view revenue.

## Scope

- apps/web/src/routes/studio/developer/* (new section gated to users with >0 listings or who opt in)
- Create listing wizard: slug, name, summary, category, repo link (optional)
- Upload version: drag-drop tarball, auto-extract manifest, show diff from previous version
- Pricing editor: choose free / one-time / subscription; set price; connect Polar product (auto-created by backend)
- Revenue dashboard: balance, recent transactions, export CSV
- Listing status flow: draft → pending review → approved/rejected → published
- Reject reasons visible to author with actionable next steps

## MVP alternative

If this feels too big for Phase 1: start with a Google Form + manual backend entry. Still gets to ~5 launch partners. Ship dev portal when we have >10 authors waiting.
acceptance_criteria:
- Author can create a listing, upload a version, set pricing, and submit for review
- Rejected listings show the reason; author can fix and resubmit
- Revenue dashboard reconciles with backend ledger

title: Docs: hosted alchemy state backend — guide + migration
description:
Documentation for the hosted backend: tradeoffs, setup, and migration from local.

## Scope

- apps/docs/content/docs/guides/hosted-state.mdx: new guide
- Cover: why hosted (team deploys, CI resilience, audit trail), why not (solo use, offline), subscription requirement, encryption model, incident/outage behavior
- Migration steps: toggle Nix option → obtain ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN → first deploy uploads existing local state
- Screenshots of the Studio State panel
- Link from deploy-*.yaml workflow comments to this doc

## Why bundle this with the feature

Users won't discover hosted state unless it's in the docs; Pro conversion depends on understanding the value.
acceptance_criteria:
- Guide builds with apps/docs
- Links from workflow comments + from the Studio empty state
- Covers pricing + how the capability token works

title: Migrate CI deploys off filesystem cache once hosted backend ships
description:
Kill the actions/cache hack in deploy-{web,docs}.yaml.

## Scope

- .github/workflows/deploy-web.yaml: remove 'Restore alchemy state' step (lines ~82-93)
- .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml: remove analogous step (lines ~80-88)
- Same for the destroy jobs
- Add ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN to the job env (pulled from GitHub secrets)
- Update comments: explain why state persistence is no longer our problem
- Verify preview deploys still work (state now survives runner churn because it's in our DB)

## Blocker

Only run this after HostedStateStore is shipped and proven on dev-CI for a week.
acceptance_criteria:
- CI workflows have no filesystem state caching
- Preview deploys + destroys work across runner churn
- bun.lock / workflow diff shows net-negative lines

title: Studio: State panel showing hosted state per workspace/stack/stage
description:
New studio UI panel for browsing hosted state — who deployed what, when, which resources exist per stage.

## Scope

- apps/web/src/components/studio/panels/state-panel.tsx
- Wires to alchemyState.list + listStages tRPC procedures (not the agent)
- Tree view: workspace → stack → stage → resources
- Row actions: 'View JSON' (decrypt on-demand via get), 'Rollback to previous version' (requires versioning; keep as a follow-up)
- Empty state for local-backend users pointing to the Pro upgrade

## Why this matters

Gives Pro users immediate visible value — audit log-like view of their cloud state they couldn't have on local filesystem.
acceptance_criteria:
- Panel lists all stages for the active workspace
- JSON view decrypts on demand and renders in a modal
- Local-backend users see upgrade prompt
- Panel doesn't break for free users (no 500s)

title: Nix option: stack.deploy.stateBackend = 'hosted' | 'local'
description:
Expose the state backend choice in .stack/config.nix so users can opt into the Pro backend declaratively.

## Scope

- nix/stackpanel/core/options/deploy.nix: new option stack.deploy.stateBackend (default 'local')
- When 'hosted': emit STACKPANEL_STATE_BACKEND=hosted into _envs/deploy, and mark ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN as required
- When 'local': leave current behavior untouched
- Preflight warning if 'hosted' but no ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN in scope
- JSON schema update for IDE intellisense (.stack/gen/schemas/)
acceptance_criteria:
- Config validates with stateBackend = 'hosted' + ALCHEMY_STATE_TOKEN present
- Missing token emits a clear preflight warning
- Default-unchanged behavior: existing users see no difference
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2: usage-metered pricing via Polar Meters
description:
Enable modules to charge by usage (e.g., 'N deploys/month', 'M agents run') in addition to flat pricing.

## Scope

- Polar Meter integration via @polar-sh SDK
- Module manifest gains optional 'meters' field declaring the usage dimensions
- Runtime: module reports usage via tRPC modules.reportUsage(slug, meter, delta) — gated by license
- Billing: Polar rolls up usage monthly; webhook events translate into revenue_event rows
- Author-side: dashboard shows usage graphs + projected revenue
- User-side: studio shows current usage with soft caps before hard billing

## Why Phase 2

One-time + subscription covers 95% of modules. Metered billing adds real complexity (rate limits, reconciliation, disputes). Ship it when we have a use case demanding it.
acceptance_criteria:
- At least one test module uses metered pricing end-to-end
- Usage events reconcile between our ledger and Polar
- User can see current usage in studio
